summary: tsliverhome: typosquat of tslib fetches and eval()s remote code on use
details: Package `tsliverhome` impersonates Microsoft's `tslib` (TypeScript runtime helpers): the README is a verbatim copy of the official tslib README referencing `github.com/microsoft/tslib`, `--importHelpers`, `__extends`, `__assign`, while `package.json` metadata is empty (`author: ""`, `description: ""`). The shipped code implements none of the advertised TypeScript helpers. Instead, `index.js` defines `uuri = "https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/"` and a helper `mreq()` that HTTP-GETs `https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/23`, then passes the response body to `eval(JSON.parse(b))` with no integrity, signature, or origin check. This `mreq()` call is reached from the package's default export `getPlugin`, the only functional export — so any consumer that imports the package and invokes it executes attacker-controlled JavaScript in the Node.js process. The destination is an anonymously-publishable Vercel preview URL (`*.vercel.app`) unrelated to any publisher identity declared by the package, and its content is mutable, so the attacker can swap the payload at any time. Combined with the typosquat + cloned README lure, this is a deliberate supply-chain trap designed to achieve remote code execution on developers who mistype `tslib`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
